layers.name: Presentation Layer
layers.responsibility: Renders UI, handles user input (clicks, keyboard), displays results, manages themes.

layers.name: Application Layer
layers.responsibility: Contains core business logic for calculations, manages application flow, orchestrates interactions between modules, handles state updates.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.responsibility: Manages global application state (current input, result, history, memory, error status, theme). Provides a single source of truth for data.

modules.name: Input Module
modules.description: Captures and processes all user input from UI buttons and keyboard, dispatches actions to the Application Layer.

modules.name: Display Module
modules.description: Responsible for rendering the current input expression, calculated result, and any error messages.

modules.name: Core Calculator Engine
modules.description: Implements the logic for parsing mathematical expressions and evaluating basic arithmetic operations (+, -, *, /). Handles order of operations and parentheses.

modules.name: Scientific Functions Module
modules.description: Provides implementations for advanced mathematical functions (sin, cos, tan, log, ln, sqrt, power, percentage, toggle sign).

modules.name: Memory Functions Module
modules.description: Manages calculator memory operations (M+, M-, MR, MC).

modules.name: History Module
modules.description: Stores, retrieves, and manages the history of calculations performed by the user.

modules.name: Theme Module
modules.description: Manages theme selection (light/dark mode) and applies appropriate styling to the UI.

modules.name: Accessibility Module
modules.description: Integrates accessibility features like ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, and focus management.

data_flow: 1. User Interaction: User interacts with the UI (clicks a button, types on keyboard).
2. Input Module: Captures the event, performs basic validation, and dispatches an action/event to the Application Layer.
3. Application Layer: Receives the action. Based on the action type:
- If '=' is pressed, it calls the 'Core Calculator Engine' to evaluate the expression.
- If a scientific function button is pressed, it calls the 'Scientific Functions Module'.
- If a memory function button is pressed, it calls the 'Memory Functions Module'.
- If an error occurs during processing, an 'error' state is set.
4. Core Calculator Engine/Scientific/Memory Modules: Perform the requested calculation/operation and return the result or an error.
5. Application Layer: Receives the result/error. Updates the 'Data Layer' (central state store) with new 'currentExpression', 'currentResult', 'history', 'memory', or 'error' status.
6. Data Layer: The central state is updated.
7. Display Module: Observes changes in the 'Data Layer' and re-renders the UI to display the updated 'currentExpression', 'currentResult', or 'error' message.

state_design.central_store: A single, immutable state tree managed by a state management library.

key_states.name: currentExpression
key_states.description: The mathematical expression currently being input or built by the user.

key_states.name: currentResult
key_states.description: The last calculated result or the intermediate result displayed.

key_states.name: history
key_states.type: Array<{ expression: string, result: string, timestamp: string }>
key_states.description: An array storing objects representing past calculations, including expression, result, and timestamp.

key_states.name: memory
key_states.description: The numeric value currently stored in the calculator's memory.

key_states.name: error
key_states.description: Stores any active error message (e.g., 'Divide by zero', 'Syntax Error'). Null if no error.

folders.name: src/assets/
folders.description: Static assets like images, icons, and fonts.

folders.name: src/components/
folders.description: Reusable UI components (e.g., buttons, display, layout, scientific panel).

folders.name: src/modules/
folders.description: Core business logic modules (e.g., calculator-engine, scientific-functions, memory-manager, history-manager).

folders.name: src/services/
folders.description: Utility services, helper functions, and API integrations if any (e.g., keyboard input handler).

folders.name: src/store/
folders.description: State management configuration, actions, reducers/slices, and selectors.

folders.name: src/styles/
folders.description: Global styles, theme definitions, and utility CSS.

folders.name: src/utils/
folders.description: General utility functions (e.g., expression parsing, formatting, validation).

folders.name: src/hooks/
folders.description: Custom React Hooks for reusable logic (if using React).

folders.name: src/index.js/ts
folders.description: The entry point of the application.

tech_stack.frontend_framework: React.js (for component-based UI and efficient rendering)
tech_stack.language: TypeScript (for type safety, improved readability, and maintainability)
tech_stack.state_management: Redux Toolkit (for centralized, predictable state management and simplified logic)
tech_stack.styling: Styled Components or Emotion (for component-scoped CSS-in-JS, theme support, and dynamic styling)
tech_stack.build_tool: Vite or Webpack (for bundling, development server, and build optimizations)
tech_stack.testing_frameworks: Jest (for unit and integration testing), React Testing Library (for UI component testing), Cypress (for end-to-end testing)
tech_stack.linting_formatting: ESLint, Prettier (for code quality and consistency)

api_spec.description: Since this is a client-side application, 'APIs' refer to the interfaces between internal modules and the state management system.
examples: calculatorEngine.evaluate(expression: string): { result: number | string, error: string | null }
scientificFunctions.calculate(funcName: string, value: number, ...args: number[]): number | string | null
memoryManager.add(value: number): void; memoryManager.subtract(value: number): void; memoryManager.recall(): number; memoryManager.clear(): void
historyManager.addEntry(entry: { expression: string, result: string, timestamp: string }): void; historyManager.getHistory(): Array<object>

api_spec.description: Defined actions to modify the central state and selectors to retrieve state values.

security.measure: Implement robust input validation for all user inputs. Strictly avoid `eval()` for expression evaluation to prevent code injection. Use a custom parsing algorithm (e.g., Shunting-yard) for mathematical expressions.
Regularly update third-party libraries and npm packages to patch known vulnerabilities. Use tools like `npm audit` or `yarn audit`.
Ensure all displayed user-generated content (e.g., history entries, if manipulable) is properly escaped to prevent rendering malicious scripts.
Implement a strict CSP to whitelist trusted sources for scripts, styles, and other resources, mitigating XSS and other injection attacks.
Proper use of ARIA attributes and semantic HTML contributes to a more robust and less exploitable UI.

performance.measure: Leverage the chosen framework's optimization techniques (e.g., React.memo, useCallback, useMemo, virtual DOM) to minimize unnecessary component re-renders and improve UI responsiveness.
Utilize tree-shaking, code splitting, and lazy loading for larger components (if applicable) to reduce the initial JavaScript bundle size and improve load times.
Optimize any static assets (e.g., icons, fonts) to reduce their file size and improve network load performance.
Design components to minimize direct DOM manipulation and rely on the framework's efficient reconciliation process.
Optimize state updates to only re-render affected components. Use selectors efficiently to prevent unnecessary recomputations.
Ensure efficient read/write operations for local storage to avoid blocking the main thread, especially for larger history sets.
